---
title: "Process Modeling and Simulation"
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMM D, YYYY"
---

Welcome to the course website for **Process Modeling and Simulation**.

This website contains the syllabus, lecture notes, assignments, examples, and resources for the course.

## Course description

This course introduces mathematical modeling and simulation techniques for chemical engineering processes. Students will learn how to formulate process models, solve algebraic and numerical problems, and implement simulations using computer-based tools.

The course covers both fundamental and advanced topics, including thermodynamic property calculations, material and energy balances, flash calculations, reactor models, distillation, sequential-modular simulation, equation-oriented simulation, process optimization, and hybrid modeling approaches.

## Main topics

- Thermodynamics I: Thermodynamic properties
- Thermodynamics II: Pure fluid
- Thermodynamics III: Mixture
- Energy Balances of multicomponent systems
- Mixer and splitter models
- Thermodynamics IV: Vapor-liquid equilibrium
- Flash calculations I: Simplified flash calculations
- Flash calculations II: Rigorous flash calculations
- Distillation I: Simplified distillation calculations
- Distillation II: Rigorous distillation calculations
- Fixed-conversion and kinetic-based reactor models
- Thermodynamics V: Chemical reaction equilibrium
- Equilibrium reactors
- Sequential modular approach for process simulation
- Equation-oriented approach for process simulation
- Process optimization
- Hybrid modeling for process simulation
